title: Puffed Zanzibari Coconut Empanadas
description: A spicy, fluffy Kenyan snack featuring lamination puff and sattvic-friendly coconut filling, perfect for kids.
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine flour and 1 tsp salt for dough.
Add cold butter pieces and cut into flour until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually pour cold water, mixing until dough just comes together; wrap and chill 30 min.
In a separate bowl, whisk coconut milk, shredded coconut, cumin, coriander, cayenne, salt, ginger, and shredded carrot until smooth.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium, pour filling liquid, and simmer until it thickens, about 8 minutes.
Remove skillet from heat and let filling cool slightly.
Divide chilled dough into 12 equal portions, each about 25g, and roll into circles about 8cm diameter.
Spoon 1-2 tbsp of filling onto each dough circle, fold into half, seal edges with a fork.
Preheat oven to 200°C and brush empanadas lightly with melted coconut oil.
Bake empanadas for 15 minutes until puffed and golden.
